command:
ssh -i /home/jose/.ssh/n8n_bridge -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 jose@192.168.0.202 bash << 'REMOTE'
echo "=== Webhook delivery status from Telegram (full detail) ==="
curl -s "https://api.telegram.org/bot8849049212:AAH2CHM_CDLDeTLfVxw7UGz5aO1weVBdnpw/getWebhookInfo" | python3 -m json.tool

echo ""
echo "=== Test the FULL public path: POST to sakura.cutegrafiks.com/telegram ==="
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "public /telegram POST: %{http_code}\n" \
  -X POST https://sakura.cutegrafiks.com/telegram \
  -H "X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token: 65b0642ca8a7fc2ae28fa8e8234895cd38fc88933dd9bf6a45157546c66b98a6" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"update_id":1,"message":{"message_id":1,"chat":{"id":-1003934415467,"type":"supergroup"},"text":"ping"}}' \
  --max-time 12 2>/dev/null

echo "=== Which cloudflared route handles sakura? Is .202:8080 the target? ==="
# The tunnel runs on .191 and .201, routing sakura->.202:8080
# Check if .202:8080 is reachable from the tunnel host .191
echo "(tunnel routes sakura.cutegrafiks.com -> 192.168.0.202:8080)"

echo "=== Gateway process detail ==="
ps aux | grep -E "hermes.*gateway" | grep -v grep | head -2

echo "=== Does gateway write ANY log anywhere? Find recent hermes files ==="
find /home/jose/.hermes -name "*.log" -newermt "-1 hour" 2>/dev/null | head -5
ls -la /home/jose/.hermes/*.log 2>/dev/null | head -5
REMOTE
